prompt:
Context: our inventory sync talks to a partner API that flaps a few times an hour, and right now we have three different copy-pasted retry loops, none of which can be cancelled, one of which once hammered the partner for 40 minutes after a deploy because nothing ever gave up. I want ONE proper helper, retry.ts, exporting retry(fn, opts), and then we delete the copies.

How it needs to behave:

- fn is called as fn({ attempt, signal }) — attempt starts at 1, signal is a per-attempt AbortSignal the fn should watch so it can bail out.
- opts: attempts (total tries, positive integer or throw TypeError synchronously), baseDelayMs, factor (default 2), maxDelayMs (cap), signal (outer AbortSignal), attemptTimeoutMs, delay.
- Backoff between failures is baseDelayMs * factor^(attempt-1), capped at maxDelayMs. Last error wins when attempts run out; non-Error rejection values must come through untouched (one of the old loops wrapped everything in a new Error and we lost the response bodies).
- Outer signal: already aborted -> reject with its reason before fn is even called. Aborted mid-attempt -> reject promptly with the reason and abort the per-attempt signal. Aborted during a backoff wait -> same, and the wait's timer must actually be cancelled.
- attemptTimeoutMs: if an attempt hangs longer than this, abort its signal and treat it as a failed attempt (retry if attempts remain); if it was the last attempt, reject with an error whose name is 'TimeoutError'. When an attempt finishes fast, the timeout timer must be cleaned up, not left running.
- opts.delay is the injectable timer: (ms, signal) => Promise<void>, resolving after ms and rejecting promptly if signal aborts. You must route ALL waiting through it (backoff waits and attempt timers), always passing a signal you abort when the wait is moot — that's how the tests keep everything deterministic, and how the default (which you also provide, built on setTimeout) avoids leaking timers.

test_retry.ts is the acceptance contract — `node --test test_retry.ts` must pass, tests stay as-is. Stdlib only.
